title: Word Definitions, Tokenization, Boundaries, and Offsets
description: Whitespace splitting treats punctuation as words, a regex drops apostrophes and non-Latin scripts, hyphen policy changes totals silently, and empty tokens enter the frequency table. Learners cumulatively produce tested word and token contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define a word for the stakeholder, language set, punctuation, identifiers, numbers, contractions, and hyphenated forms before counting.
Compare whitespace, regular-expression, Unicode-boundary, dictionary, and language-tailored segmentation against labeled cases.
Specify leading, trailing, internal, curly-apostrophe, dash, and repeated-punctuation behavior with changed examples.
